finding: April 11 (Saturday) stands out as an extreme intensity day: 36 sessions, approximately 3098 user messages, spanning from 08:18 to past 23:37 with work continuing into the early hours. April 12 (Sunday) continued at similar intensity with 33 sessions and 2244 messages. The combined weekend output of ~5300 messages across ~69 sessions suggests manic-mode hyperfocus. Projects jhana, weekend, and claude-marketplace were the primary targets, with rapid context-switching between them throughout the day.
existing_mitigation: A late-night-warn.py hook exists that warns after 20:00 with 5+ hours of session time, but the pattern shows sessions continued well past those warnings on both days.

finding: Late-night sessions (past 23:00) appear on at least 5 of the 14 active days in the analysis window: Apr 7 (5 sessions past 23:00 including one running to 00:01), Apr 10 (session at 23:24), Apr 11 (5 sessions past 23:00 running to 23:37+), Apr 14 (sessions extending past midnight into Apr 15 at 00:29). This is a recurring pattern rather than an isolated late night, suggesting difficulty disengaging from work in the evening. The existing late-night-warn hook fires at 20:00 but does not appear to prevent the pattern from continuing.
existing_mitigation: late-night-warn.py hook in settings.json (UserPromptSubmit). It warns after 20:00 with 5+ hours of active time, but the pattern persists across multiple days.
